Mayo v Cork

Match Analysis

Mayo v Cork

Possession-by-possession Analysis
10 July 2026
WINNERS
Mayo
0-23
FULL TIME
Mayo
by 5
Cork
0-18

Mayo won despite being well behind in possessions. Cork's wasteful shooting was their undoing. 

Cork's shot accuracy was only 48%  (-6pts vs. xP). On kick-outs, Mayo retained just 53% of their own restarts to Cork’s 67%. Final: Mayo 0-23 to Cork 0-18.

1

Overview

How the match was won

From possessions to points: the butterfly chart shows how each team translated their ball into scores. Mayo lived off scraps, but were more efficient in front of goal. Cork on the other hand were extremely wasteful.  

Mayo
Cork
2

Possession Flow

Mayo's failure to win many Cork kickouts put them on the back foot. Cork's 17 misses let them off the hook.

Reading it: every possession by what it produced — Mayo 38 to Cork’s 44. Misses and turnovers (grey) make up a large share of each.

3

How the Lead Changed

The score margin across the game. Above the line, Mayo lead; below the line, Cork lead. Mayo built a lead early in the second half and held on until the end. 

Half-time: Mayo 0-09 · Cork 0-09.

4

Key Phase of Play

Early in the second half, Mayo went from 1 down to 4 up, thanks in part to two 2-pointers from Mayo's Darragh Byrne.

Score (1/2/G)Wide / short / saved / turnoverKick-out wonBall direction · colour = team
How to read this

Reading it: each column is one possession. The chip on the halfway line shows how the ball was won, in the colour of the team that won it. The bold arrow traces the attack toward goal; scores and wides line up on the goal line. A turnover or short box stretches into the next column where the opponent’s possession runs on from it.

5

Kick-Out Battle

Each team’s own kick-outs, by where the ball was won. Mayo allowed Cork to win a huge amount of possession from short kick-outs. Mayo went long most of the time where it was a lottery. 

Kick-out returns by length iNet score is the points a team scored directly from its own kick-outs minus the points conceded directly from them — counting only the possession that began with the kick-out. Short = ball won inside the own 45 m line; long = beyond it.

Where Possession Was Lost

Turnovers conceded with each team attacking up the pitch. Both teams lost a similar number of turnovers, but Cork conceded more from them (8pts vs. 5pts for Mayo)

Only open-play turnovers — lost in midfield or in the opposition half — are counted; shots dropped short or off the post are misses, not turnovers.

7

Scoring & Expected Points

Every shot by location, each team attacking up its own pitch. Marker size scales with expected score; diamonds are goals, hollow rings off target. Cork were extremely wasteful in front of goal. 15 of their 1-point attempts were missed as well as both goal chances, with Mayo's Jack Livingstone, puliing off two great saves.
